title: Luffy’s Grief and the ASL Past: Rising Conflict
description: In the Post-War / ASL Flashback storyline, this section centers on Unknown person #3, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #13, Luffy as the scene develops across pages 5–8.
scene: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Unknown person #3, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #13, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: rising conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: You promised to only see Luffy

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She's come down with a terrible virus!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But... Impel down, mearinguarters!!! He truly was like an ant in the midst of a great storm!!! It was practically an act of God that he returned with his life... just do the heavens want with this man!!??

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Where did jimbei go with him...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But it might be worth risking it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: You think there are any girl bears?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I couldn't let you save me...

summary: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Unknown person #3, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #13, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: rising conflict.
